title: viola arvensis var. ruralis
intro: Viola arvensis var. ruralis, also known as the woodland violet, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the common dog violet, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and purple flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Viola arvensis var. ruralis include relief from anxiety and insomnia, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
